title: Facebook app keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don't need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Facebook App Secret detected in variable assignment. Facebook App Secrets are the master credentials for your Facebook application and must never be exposed. With the App Secret, attackers can generate any type of access token for any user who has authorized your app, bypass OAuth flows, sign forged API requests, access all user data your app can see, and completely compromise your application. Reset immediately in the Facebook App Dashboard at developers.facebook.com.

Facebook App Secret detected (alphanumeric format). Facebook app secrets grant complete control over your Facebook application including generating tokens, accessing user data, and signing API requests. Exposing this credential compromises all users who have authorized your app. Reset immediately at developers.facebook.com/apps.

Facebook App ID detected in variable assignment. While App IDs are semi-public identifiers, they are often found alongside App Secrets in the same configuration. The App ID alone identifies your application and is used in conjunction with the App Secret for API authentication. Ensure no App Secret is exposed in the same file or nearby configuration.
